title: Venice Biennale of Architecture - Wikipedia
body: Biennial architecture expo in Italy Venice Biennale of Architecture Biennale Architettura Genre Biennale , architecture Frequency Biennial, odd-numbered years since 2021 (even-numbered years 2000–2018) Locations Venice , Italy Inaugurated 1980 ; 46 years ago ( 1980 ) Most recent 2025 Next event 2027 Website www .labiennale .org /en /architecture / The Venice Biennale of Architecture ( Italian : Mostra di Architettura di Venezia ) is an international exhibition showcasing architectural works from around the world, held in Venice, Italy, every other year. [ 1 ] Originally held in even-numbered years until 2018, the event was postponed in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic and rescheduled for 2021, shifting the calendar to odd-numbered years. It forms the architecture section of the broader Venice Biennale and was officially established in 1980, although architecture had been included in the Venice Art Biennale since 1968. [ 1 ] The Biennale is divided into two main sections: the permanent national pavilions located in the Giardini della Biennale , and the Arsenale , which hosts projects from numerous countries under one roof. History [ edit ] In 1975, during the presidency of Carlo Ripa di Meana, the first initiative towards an architecture exhibition was taken with the Exhibition «A proposito del Mulino Stucky», curated by Vittorio Gregotti . In 1980, the Architecture department was established during the presidency of Giuseppe Galasso, who appointed Paolo Portoghesi as its first director. [ 2 ] Exhibitions [ edit ] Main article: List of Venice Biennale of Architecture exhibitions Awards [ edit ] Main articles: List of winners of the Golden Lion for Best National Participation at the Venice Biennale of Architecture and List of winners of the Golden Lion for Best Participation in the International Exhibition at the Venice Biennale of Architecture See also [ edit ] List of architecture prizes Asplund Pavilion Nordic Pavilion American Pavilion List of Chilean pavilions at the Venice Biennale of Architecture References [ edit ] ^ a b Rosenfield, Karissa (28 August 2012). "A History of the Venice Architecture Biennale" . ArchDaily . Retrieved 23 April 2025 . ^ "Biennale Architettura 2023 | Archive" . La Biennale di Venezia . 26 April 2017 . Retrieved 23 April 2025 . Further reading [ edit ] Levy, Aaron; Menking, William, eds. (2010). Architecture on Display: On the History of the Venice Biennale of Architecture . London: AA Publications. ISBN 978-1-902902-96-8 .
